channel.title: The New Yorker — Reporting
channel.description: Latest reporting from The New Yorker

item.title: Robyn, on Her Own
item.description: The pop star brings motherhood and middle age to the dance floor.

item.title: Does A.I. Need a Constitution?
item.description: A new set of precepts is meant to make the chatbot Claude wise, decent, and safe. It also marks a striking transfer of public responsibility from constitutional government to private tech firms.

item.title: He Helped Stop Iran from Getting the Bomb
item.description: A former C.I.A. officer says that he recruited scientists as part of the United States’ effort to disrupt Iran’s nuclear program.

item.title: How I Became a Filmmaker
item.description: After college, I joined an odd little utopia of movie nerds working out of an office on lower Broadway. Then the sustainability of the setup started to seem questionable.

item.title: At Synagogues, Tensions Are Boiling Over
item.description: Disagreements about Gaza and Zionism have divided congregations.

item.title: The Camps Promising to Turn You—or Your Son—Into an Alpha Male
item.description: At the Men of War Crucible, you bear-crawl through rivers. At Warrior Week, you dig your own grave. At the Squire Program, your teen-ager can take part, too.

item.title: Why Are People Injecting Themselves with Peptides?
item.description: Health and wellness influencers are hawking unapproved treatments on the gray market. The future of the F.D.A.—and the health of consumers—is at stake.

item.title: Can Sponge Cities Save Us from the Coming Floods?
item.description: As the planet gets warmer and the rains fall harder, the future of flood control is looking less like a wall and something more like a park.

item.title: Sam Altman May Control Our Future—Can He Be Trusted?
item.description: New interviews and closely guarded documents shed light on the persistent doubts about the head of OpenAI.

item.title: How the Internet Fringe Infiltrated Republican Politics
item.description: Inside the battle for the post-MAGAG.O.P.
